body.title: CLOSED: Build skills-sync skill — ~/.claude/skills ↔ repo canonical copy
body.result: skills-sync/SKILL.md built — wraps agentctl skills deploy/status, batch consent, bidirectional (repo→local + local→repo), discovers repo path via DEFAULT_MASTER_ROOT import (commit fea2166 on agent_datacenter)
body.description:
No mechanism exists to keep ~/.claude/skills/ in sync with the canonical repo copy at ${AGENT_DATACENTER_HOME}/skills/. Skills drift across machines, improvements don't propagate to swarm boxes, and the swarm can't share procedural patterns (engrams). Build a skills-sync skill: list all skills, compare mtime/content, show summary of repo-newer vs local-newer, prompt for BATCH consent (not per-file), apply on consent. Future: swarm P2P engram exchange builds on this same mechanism.

**Affected files:** New ~/.claude/skills/skills-sync/SKILL.md; new ${AGENT_DATACENTER_HOME}/skills/skills-sync/ (canonical copy added to repo).
**Design rules:** Batch-consent pattern (no per-file prompts per user requirement). Uses AGENT_DATACENTER_HOME from T-cc-workflow-tools-env-var.
**Scope boundary:** IN: local to repo sync both directions with consent, diff display. OUT: swarm P2P sync (future), auto-sync without consent, skill validation/testing.
**Test plan:** Run skill with repo and local in sync -> 'nothing to sync'; modify one local skill, run -> shows local-newer with diff; modify one repo skill, run -> shows repo-newer; accept -> file updated.
**Pass condition:** After agentctl init on a new machine, running /skills-sync pulls latest skills from repo and installs them in ~/.claude/skills/ in one batch action.
